taxonomy: Octopoma inclusum is a species of flowering plant in the family Aizoaceae. This succulent plant is native to South Africa and is characterized by its small, green leaves and yellow flowers. The classification of Octopoma inclusum includes the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, and the order Caryophyllales. Further classification places the plant in the family Aizoaceae, which is a group of succulent plants commonly known as the ice plants. The genus Octopoma also contains other species of succulents that are native to the same region.
uses: Octopoma inclusum is a succulent plant species native to South Africa, and although limited information is available on its medicinal uses, it is often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. Some sources suggest that Octopoma inclusum has potential in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties.
